title: leucanthemum vulgare subsp. montserratianum
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. montserratianum, also known as Montserrat's oxeye daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its bright yellow center and white petals. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and other minor health issues. The health benefits of Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. montserratianum include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce fever, and promote skin health. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
